name: Ted Buckner
abstract: Theodore Guy Buckner (December 14, 1913, St. Louis, Missouri - April 12, 1976, Detroit, Michigan) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist and flutist.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Music of Tommy Dorsey and McKinney's Cotton Pickers

works.t: Prelude in A Major, Op. 28, No. 7
Baby, Won't You Please Come Home
Will You, Won't You, Be My Babe?

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Ted Buckner... came into the band. I was playing the lead alto... We'll split the book.

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: But we went in that band together. I never forgot that. It was September, '37.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Ted Buckner and his brother both were fantastic musicians around there at that time... Ted Buckner played the xylophones with four mallets

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: As recently as 1975 I made some records with J. C. and Doc Cheatham, with Ted Buckner before he died
